title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Mutsu presents a brown paper envelope containing a threatening note constructed from mismatched newspaper clippings. The note warns, "Death is lurking in the city of Sōyama." Murakami observes that the sender either knows him intimately or knows nothing about him at all, while the narrator agrees to accompany Murakami, jokingly expressing a desire to meet the author of such a note.

The investigation shifts to a dark, windowless, cylindrical stone tower that resembles a giant well. The space is illuminated only by faint light from high arrow slits and an ancient, unlit oil lamp. Inside, a gruesome scene is discovered: the decapitated head of Ito lies near a headless corpse. The decapitation is so precise that the head and body appear to belong together. Kusaka suggests the body might belong to Haya. A search of the corpse's pockets reveals only a black leather wallet, a room key, and a floral handkerchief. In the corner of the room stands a dark brown guillotine featuring a glowing, one-meter-wide blade.

As Hodai leads a pale Kikumura away to contact the forensic team, the group discusses a potential deception involving someone imitating Yamazaki over the phone. Using a lamp, Mukai reveals ancient script inscribed on the cobblestones in the center of the room: "Mene, mene, tekel, upharsin." Mukai explains this is a biblical quote from the Book of Daniel regarding the fall of a kingdom, suggesting the crimes are the work of an "invisible hand of God." Woodside notes that the marble door is the only entrance and can be locked from the outside, leading the narrator to conclude that the scene is a locked-room mystery.
